title: Women at the Helm: Redefining India’s Development Story
content:
India’s development narrative has decisively shifted from empowering women to being powered by women. From classrooms to research labs, women are rapidly expanding their presence in STEM, with 43% enrolment and growing representation in higher education.

Policy initiatives like the Nari Shakti Vandan Adhiniyam and programs such as Lakhpati Didi are strengthening women’s roles in governance and economic growth. Financial inclusion through Jan Dhan Yojana and rising workforce participation further highlight this transformation.

However, challenges like unpaid care work, limited access to capital, and workplace inequality continue to hinder progress. Addressing these gaps is essential to unlock the full potential of women in India’s development journey.

Women-led development is no longer a goal—it is the foundation of India’s path toward Viksit Bharat 2047.

title: Strengthening Democratic Institutions
content: Strong institutions are essential for transparency, accountability and governance.
